the sim opens on Viper, which the field rarely builds (1.4% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Serath (it never builds Storm Breaker, Spectra) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +14.7% on the burst combo at minute 15.

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: On-Hit Drain
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for lifesteal sustain, online around minute 24 — about 257 HP drained back over a 10s fight in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: On-Hit Brawler
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 24 — about 645 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.3× the extended fights vs bruisers: try Viper over Sky Splitter (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: On-Hit Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 500 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.0× the 10s rotation damage: try Viper over Sky Splitter (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their burst combo hits 26% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Aegis Of Agawar instead of Augmentation: their burst combo hits 10% less hard, and this kit gives up 12% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Rampage deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Aegis Of Agawar instead of Augmentation: their burst combo hits 10% less hard, and this kit gives up 12% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Feng Mao deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their burst combo hits 26% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Augmentation: their burst combo hits 17% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Yin's main threat is Backlash; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their burst combo hits 27% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

metaBuilds.title: Physical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 4,603 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: On-Hit DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 25 — about 355 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Aegis Of Agawar instead of Spear Of Desolation: their burst combo hits 17% less hard, and this kit gives up 16% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spear Of Desolation: their burst combo hits 33% less hard, and this kit gives up 28% of its damage for it.
